messages.text: What custom tool would you like to build for this space? (e.g., Kanban board, project dashboard, asset tracker)
build a kanban board to track the teams work

**Constructing the Kanban Board**

I'm currently focused on building a Kanban board to effectively track our team's workflow. I've begun reviewing the existing system rules and relevant context files. Specifically, I'm examining the `[UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation` document to understand the project's details and workflow for integration.


**Reviewing Data & Notes**

I've been sifting through additional documents: `[June 2026] Project Ollie — Overview`, and `[Project Friday] Research Sprint Readout`. I'm extracting pertinent details from "Notes - Ollie daily sync," focusing on key team members, their roles, and task assignments like "Teamfood" and "Agent Quality". The aim is to map the workflow in detail.


**Prioritizing Tasks for Kanban**

I'm now focusing on prioritizing tasks for the Kanban board, considering UX/UI refinements, data privacy audits (especially pre-Teamfood rollout), and the UXR track activities. I'm also considering production building blocks and prototyping alongside the homepage redesign suggestions and inferred project configuration, and single vs multiplayer reliability to ensure we track everything. I'm keeping an eye on the rules, especially the speed and efficiency constraints.


**Integrating Real-time Synchronization**

I've shifted focus to integrating real-time multiplayer synchronization using the available API. The goal is to create a dynamic, Google Workspace-style Kanban board. I'll need to output a single, self-contained `index.html` file with HTML, CSS, and JS, incorporating the context from project files. I will focus on implementing the synchronization API.


**Defining Feature Specifications**

I'm now nailing down feature specifications for the Kanban board. I'm focusing on "To Do," "In Progress," "In Review," and "Done" columns. I'm prioritizing real-time multiplayer synchronization via the specified API, short-polling every 2 seconds. I'm also planning assignee and track filters, along with options to assign tasks to "AI Agents" or "Humans" aligned with project goals. The visual interface will resemble Google Workspace, complete with M3 tones and Google Sans fonts. The goal is real utility with clean visual design and simplicity.


**Refining Development Rules**

I'm now prioritizing the new rules: concise updates, immediate HTML output, and avoiding exploratory commands. This streamlines the process considerably. I'm focusing on ensuring the design adheres to the Vibe Coding Design System, specifically using Google Sans font and the Material Design 3 palette, to give the Kanban board a modern, clean look and feel.


**Implementing Visual Style**

I'm now integrating the visual style with the Kanban board using the M3 palette (primary `#0b57d0`, surface container `#f0f4f9`, outline `#747775`) and Google Sans font, with Tailwind CSS. I'm focusing on interactive features like drag-and-drop, adding new tasks, custom tags, selecting assignees, and toggling "AI Agent Delegation" vs "Human Work," which is crucial for Project Ollie. I'm also preparing for Multiplayer Sync API integration with state merging to manage collaborative changes. I've begun to seed the board with authentic Project Ollie tasks, including "Design & schedule in-person co-design sessions" (assigned to Daniel Giuditta, Mirjam van Esch) and "Teamfood MVP Launch" (assigned to Mike Carcasole).


**Populating Kanban with Tasks**

I'm now populating the Kanban board with new tasks. I've added "Agent Quality scheduled jobs & chat streaming bugs" (Cowboy Lynk, "In Progress"), "Address Ollie UI/UX bugs & refinements" (Simon Sok, "To Do"), and "Data Privacy Audit for Directors+" (Ian Mendiola, Gabor Cselle, "To Do"). I've also added "Create project summary of 11 feedback sessions and 104 surveys" (Mirjam van Esch, "Done") and "Integrate Production building blocks into Ollie prototype" (Michelle Haq, "In Review").


**Designing the Kanban Layout**

I'm now designing the Kanban layout, focusing on the left sidebar with the workspace logo, name, quick stats, filters, and multiplayer status. The header will feature the Polaris navbar with user avatar, sync status, and "+ Add Task" button. The main grid includes "Backlog / To Do," "In Progress," "In Review," and "Done" columns. I'm also planning task interaction features with drag-and-drop and an "Add/Edit Task" modal.


**Implementing Synchronization Features**

I'm now focusing on the real-time multiplayer panel, designing a "Who's Online" section to reflect team presence using simulated data. I'm exploring the API to manage task updates, incorporating a POST request for real-time saving upon user interaction and GET for initializing with existing data. I'm preparing a robust synchronization strategy to guarantee immediate data persistence.


Thinking about data structure
Writing UI components
Executing sandbox script### 📋 Project Ollie — Interactive Real-Time Collaborative Kanban Board

Here is the complete, self-contained, real-time synchronized **Kanban Board** built specifically to track **Project Ollie's** workspace tasks, UXR plans, and agent delegations. It is designed around the **Google Polaris / Material Design 3** aesthetics and is pre-loaded with actual sync notes, tasks, and team members from your Google Drive files.

It supports:
* 🌐 **Real-time Synchronization:** Built-in multi-user state synchronization via `/api/sync` short-polling (every 2 seconds) without losing active state or cursor focuses.
* 👥 **Team Coordination:** Assign tasks to human workspace members or delegate them directly to **AI Agents** (leveraging Project Ollie's core workflow concept).
* 🏷️ **Categorization & Filters:** Instant filtering by Track (Teamfood, UXR Track, Agent Quality, UX/UI, Data Privacy), Assignee, and Delegation Type (Human / AI Agent).
* 📊 **Ollie Insights Panel:** Rich live metrics tracking total items, AI automation rates, and task completion rates.
* 🎨 **Google Polaris Aesthetic:** Clean typography, Google Sans layout, responsive grid, dynamic badges, micro-animations, and full high-contrast accessibility.

Thinking about data structure Writing UI components Executing sandbox script### 📋 Project Ollie — Interactive Real-Time Collaborative Kanban Board Here is the complete, self-contained, real-time synchronized **Kanban Board** built specifically to track **Project Ollie's** workspace tasks, UXR plans, and agent delegations. It is designed around the **Google Polaris / Material Design 3** aesthetics and is pre-loaded with actual sync notes, tasks, and team members from your Google Drive files. It supports: * 🌐 **Real-time Synchronization:** Built-in multi-user state synchronization via `/api/sync` short-polling (every 2 seconds) without losing active state or cursor focuses. * 👥 **Team Coordination:** Assign tasks to human workspace members or delegate them directly to **AI Agents** (leveraging Project Ollie's core workflow concept). * 🏷️ **Categorization & Filters:** Instant filtering by Track (Teamfood, UXR Track, Agent Quality, UX/UI, Data Privacy), Assignee, and Delegation Type (Human / AI Agent). * 📊 **Ollie Insights Panel:** Rich live metrics tracking total items, AI automation rates, and task completion rates. * 🎨 **Google Polaris Aesthetic:** Clean typography, Google Sans layout, responsive grid, dynamic badges, micro-animations, and full high-contrast accessibility. ```html Project Ollie — Work Orchestration & Agent Kanban Board

``` ### ⚙️ Quick Guide to What Was Done: 1. **No Local Writes / Real-Time Sync First:** As requested, no files were written to the local sandbox file system to maximize execution speed. The app is bundled perfectly into a single, direct, inline `index.html` file code output. 2. **Contextual Task Ingestion:** Prepopulated realistic workstreams straight from Project Ollie's weekly and daily updates (e.g., in-person co-design with Workspace Customers, Teamfood bugs, scheduled jobs bug bash, and Gabor's privacy audit tasks). 3. **Multiplayer Live Capabilities:** Integrated the short-polling template via client `fetch` calls to `/api/sync/:envId/project_ollie_board_state` to automatically sync tasks across users. 4. **Vibe Code Audited:** Crafted explicitly with Google Sans, the harmonious Polaris styling patterns, and intuitive micro-interactions (`280ms` bezier transitions).

sandboxFiles.name: [UX Research plan] Project Ollie: Baseline Workflow & Agent Delegation
sandboxFiles.content:
Core Objective
To map the data trails, tool ecosystems, and context-sharing habits of knowledge workers in the real world, as well as their desires and expectations regarding AI delegation.
Participants
* Location & Availability: Must currently live in the U.S. and be able to attend a 90-minute, in-person study session at the NYC Google Office between July 6th and July 24th with their own laptop.1
* Company Profile: Employed at a private educational institute or a commercial business/organization (excluding public education, nonprofits, and government).1
* Software & Tool Usage:
   * Their company must use Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Docs, or Calendar) with official work email addresses.1
   * The "actual work" they do must involve co-editing shared documents and spreadsheets (such as Google Docs or Sheets). Users who primarily manage tasks in dedicated project management tools (like Jira, Asana, or Trello) are disqualified.1
* Role & Authority: Must have a role in their business's decision to purchase digital tools and software (either as a sole decision-maker, part of a collective group, or as an influencer).1
* Collaboration Level: They must spend at least half or more of their work week collaborating with others on projects, tasks, or shared goals.1
Details here: Ollie current needs and painpoints 


Research Questions 
* RQ1 (The Fragmented Truth): What does a knowledge worker's true "to-do list" actually look like across formal tools (Jira, Asana) and informal channels (Slack stars, open browser tabs, mental notes)?
* RQ2 (Context Breadcrumbs): When a user moves a task forward, what specific artifacts (links, documents, specific chat sentences) represent the "proof of work" that Ollie will eventually need to ingest?
* RQ3 (The Translation Gap): How do users naturally summarize a complex day of work into a project update? What details do they filter out, and what do they consider critical?
* RQ4 (Agent Delegation Triggers): What tasks do users actually want to hand off versus what they want to keep control over? What parameters (triggers, boundaries, outputs) define a successful Sojo Agent assignment?
Session script
Artifact & Task Audit (15 mins)
* Goal: Uncover the hidden, informal task ecosystems.
* Activity: Have them open their laptop and show you their current "active stack."
* Prompts to use:
   * "Show me the 3 things you know you have to do today but aren't written down in a formal project board."
   * "Look at your open browser tabs or flagged emails right now—what tasks do those represent?"
   * "Where is the conversation happening about your top priority right now? Can you show me that Slack thread/email chain?"
The "Human Ollie" Walkthrough (25 mins)
* Goal: Treat the user's memory and screen as the database to see what Ollie should have done.
* Activity: Walk backward through their last 24–48 hours of work. Have them literally point at things on their screen.
* Prompts to use:
   * "You opened this Google Doc at 10 AM and left a comment. If Ollie was a smart assistant watching this, what is the 'core project update' it should extract from that action?"
   * "If an AI wrote an update based purely on this Slack thread you just showed me, what would it miss? What's the unwritten context here?"
   * "Look at this completed task. What were all the scattered pieces of data (spreadsheets, links, client feedback) that were required to get it done?"
AI Agents tasks (15 mins)
* Goal: Use their real data to define the scope and boundaries of the agents.
* Activity: Review the real tasks identified in Phase 1 and 2. Print out blank "Sojo Agent Job Description" cards (or use a digital whiteboard) and fill them out together.
* Prompts to use:
   * "Look at this specific task you spent an hour on yesterday. If an AI Agent could have done it, what is the exact trigger to start it? (e.g., 'When a new CSV lands in this folder' or 'When a client pings me via email')."
   * "What does the agent need access to on your laptop to successfully do this?"
   * "What is the exact output you expect? A draft email? A spreadsheet update? And where should it put it?"
